title: Secure beneficiary + adoption plan (verifiedNeed flips true)
context: Elyos measures success as delivered-not-merged. A named beneficiary — a public-health program, advocacy org, newsroom, library, or OER channel — must agree to adopt/use a specific deliverable before the project reaches its Definition of Shipped. Until a beneficiary is named, verifiedNeed remains false on all tasks. This task formalizes the beneficiary acquisition outcome: documents the named partner, their commitment, and the adoption plan. The build-vs-mothball rule applies: if no beneficiary is confirmed by 2027-03-31, the project pivots to an OER/open-data channel as a reference deed or mothballs to maintenance-only. Gated on having deliverables ready (data dictionary 010, explainers 015, dashboard 017).
objective: Secure a named beneficiary organization that commits to adopting or using at least one project deliverable; document their commitment and an adoption plan; flip verifiedNeed to true for the relevant tasks; record the build-vs-mothball outcome decision if no beneficiary is confirmed by 2027-03-31.
acceptanceCriteria: A named beneficiary organization is secured: a public-health program, patient-advocacy org, newsroom, library, OER channel, or open-data portal that has confirmed they will review and use at least one specific project deliverable
Beneficiary commitment is documented: organization name, contact, the specific deliverable(s) they will adopt, their intended use, and any conditions stated
A steward is assigned and named: the person who will own the beneficiary relationship, track adoption, and record the adoption-evidence event

Adoption plan documents: how the beneficiary will receive the deliverable, how adoption will be evidenced (citation in outreach materials, published article, course module, etc.), and the evidence-recording responsibility
verifiedNeed flips to true in the relevant task JSON files once this task is complete and the beneficiary is confirmed

If no beneficiary is confirmed by 2027-03-31: the build-vs-mothball decision is recorded in governance — either pivot to an OER/open-data channel as a reference deed (dataset + toolkit deposited with provenance) or mothball to maintenance-only; the decision is documented and cannot be deferred past the deadline
output: A committed adoption plan document naming the beneficiary, their commitment, steward assignment, adoption evidence plan, and evidence-recording approach; verifiedNeed updated to true in relevant task JSONs; or, if deadline passed without a beneficiary, the build-vs-mothball decision recorded in governance.
